slides.title: Updated Parameterized SIGNED ASN.1 Type for X.509 (PKIX)

chat.author: Michael Richardson
chat.text: rfc10001 is not yet issued.  Probably someone in AUTH48 knows what it will be, but LAMPS could still, in theory, grab 10001.

chat.author: Flo D
chat.text: Apparently RFC10001 is due to be draft-ietf-dnsop-3901bis (or what that will become)

chat.author: Flo D
chat.text: https://queue.rfc-editor.org/final-review/rfc10001/

chat.author: Tadahiko Ito
chat.text:
we got smallest 5 digits prime number one. 
https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc10007.html

chat.author: Mike Ounsworth
chat.text:
Michael Richardson said:

rfc10001 is not yet issued.  Probably someone in AUTH48 knows what it will be, but LAMPS could still, in theory, grab 10001.

Instructions inclear. Is that RFC 17 (base 10) ?

chat.author: Tim Hollebeek
chat.text: Going forward, all LAMPS RFC numbers need to be a product of two primes (j/k)

chat.author: Alicja Kario
chat.text: @Mike: :+1:

chat.author: Guiliano Lehmann
chat.text: RFC 9684

chat.author: Russ Housley
chat.text: RATS CMW is published as RFC 9999

chat.author: Bas Westerbaan
chat.text:
Tim Hollebeek said:

Going forward, all LAMPS RFC numbers need to be a product of two primes (j/k)

Would love that. 10001, 10003, 10006, 10015, 10018, 10019, 10021, 10022, 10027, 10029, 10031, 10033, 10041, 10042, 10046, 10049, 10055, 10057, ...

chat.author: Tim Hollebeek
chat.text: hah! I was just generating the same list.

chat.author: Thom Wiggers
chat.text: is nonce the right name then?

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: So we can have a dog bark the RFC numbers so they are bollocks? :)

chat.author: Thom Wiggers
chat.text: if it can carry structured / not-nonce data

chat.author: Mike Ounsworth
chat.text:
Thom Wiggers said:

is nonce the right name then?

attestation-metadata-req / attestation-metadata-resp ?

chat.author: Thom Wiggers
chat.text: e.g

chat.author: Bas Westerbaan
chat.text: And if we update TLS 1.3 again, we should ensure it's rfc15246 so it looks like TLS 1.2 for those that don't know about 5 number RFCs.

chat.author: Britta Hale
chat.text:
Tim Hollebeek said:

Going forward, all LAMPS RFC numbers need to be a product of two primes (j/k)

But only for the next few years. After that you will need to generate RFC numbers based on matrices....

chat.author: Carl Wallace
chat.text:
Thom Wiggers said:

if it can carry structured / not-nonce data

neverminding that it's not used "once"

chat.author: Thom Wiggers
chat.text: those RFCs will be too large to transmit, we need isogenies

chat.author: Mike Ounsworth
chat.text:
Britta Hale said:

Tim Hollebeek said:

Going forward, all LAMPS RFC numbers need to be a product of two primes (j/k)

But only for the next few years. After that you will need to generate RFC numbers based on matrices....

I prefer seeds :upside_down:

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: just ship the seed and the full RFC number.. then if you don't verify for consistency you'll implement TLS 1.2 instead of 1.4

chat.author: Thom Wiggers
chat.text: :cry:

chat.author: Sophie Schmieg
chat.text: Ipd stands for initial private draft

chat.author: Russ Housley
chat.text: initial public draft?

chat.author: Thom Wiggers
chat.text: skip 4 just to troll people

chat.author: Thom Wiggers
chat.text: Russ: yeah NIST drafts are numbered XXX ipd

chat.author: Russ Housley
chat.text: content-type and message-digest are REQUIRED if the signed attributes are present (in RFC 5280)

chat.author: Mike Ounsworth
chat.text: @DKG -- isn't that why he's bumping the protocol version to v4?

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: would old verifiers be able to verify v4 messages?  (asking as an S/MIME novice)

chat.author: Michael StJohns
chat.text: How. Uch enveloped data in the wild?

chat.author: Michael StJohns
chat.text: Much

chat.author: Russ Housley
chat.text: All current S/MIME implementations use EnvelopedData

chat.author: Michael StJohns
chat.text: So… maybe not an option?

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: again, seems like a two-phase process: require for generators,  and later require for consumers

chat.author: Russ Housley
chat.text: Can't do AES-GCM with EnvelopedData

chat.author: Sophie Schmieg
chat.text: MTI is a cruel joke to create more emails on IETF lists

chat.author: Yoav Nir
chat.text: (insert a "implement all the PQ algorithms" meme here)

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: (sorry, my comment was about envelopedData, not about PQ)

chat.author: Michael StJohns
chat.text: Dkg - one tool implements both sides.

chat.author: David Benjamin
chat.text: Our specs are too broadly used and too slow-updating for MTI to be very useful, alas.

chat.author: Michael StJohns
chat.text: So phasing is per implementation not per side

chat.author: Bas Westerbaan
chat.text: There is value in MTI, but perhaps good to discuss it completely separately without having things block on it.

chat.author: Michael StJohns
chat.text: Hmm.  Signing. Vs encrypting.

chat.author: Yoav Nir
chat.text: https://imgflip.com/i/ax9124

chat.author: Mike Ounsworth
chat.text: We can write all the MTI text we want: this still doesn't prevent implementers from needing to do interop-testing with other vendors.

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: or they just don't. and you get some mail you can't read..

chat.author: Deb Cooley
chat.text: getting email you can't read is user friction.

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: like HTML email?

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: (ducks)

chat.author: Bas Westerbaan
chat.text: (I wish there'd be more emails I can't read.)

chat.author: Deb Cooley
chat.text: yeah, well.... if we kill email, then we'd all be happy.

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: :wait_one_second:

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: only if we replace email with a non-centralized universal messaging system

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: just use IRC :)

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: I do!  but other people don't.

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: (plus IRC isn't really federated)

chat.author: Tim Hollebeek
chat.text: Let's just all post our email content on a public blockchain

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: :scream:

chat.author: Mike Ounsworth
chat.text:
@Daniel Van Geest just said "I punt this to ASN.1 experts greater than I".
I just want to point out that this WG is supposed to be the most ASN.1-literate WG within the IETF, and yet, we still don't feel like we're experts. (and I'm with Dan, I also absolutely do not grok ASN.1). There is definitely a problem burried somewhere here.

chat.author: Tim Hollebeek
chat.text: Yes, and that problem is ASN.1

chat.author: Tim Hollebeek
chat.text: Unlike the previous comment, this time I'm actually serious

chat.author: Ben S
chat.text: ASN.2 anyone?

chat.author: Mike Ounsworth
chat.text:
Ben S said:

ASN.2 anyone?

https://datatracker.ietf.org/wg/cbor

chat.author: Tim Hollebeek
chat.text: Yep, C509 is an improvement

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: i think this WG is the WG most willing to (roll up our sleeves/hold our noses) to deal with ASN.1 -- other WGs have been happy to move on to other generic serialization formats.

chat.author: Bas Westerbaan
chat.text: We're adding another dimension in choosing which composite to pick

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: that clearly doesn't mean we're experts.

chat.author: Bas Westerbaan
chat.text: Cursed

chat.author: Flo D
chat.text:
Bas Westerbaan said:

Cursed

FN-DSA+LMS or ASN.1?

chat.author: Bas Westerbaan
chat.text: Combined they're not that small compared to ML-DSA-44

chat.author: Bas Westerbaan
chat.text:
Flo D said:

FN-DSA+LMS or ASN.1?

Yes

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: FN-DSA+LMS+ASN.1

chat.author: Kris Kwiatkowski
chat.text: Let's mix all of them

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: if this is for high value things, why define 3

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: just define high

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: Bob++

chat.author: Thom Wiggers
chat.text: Send NIST emails that you want to see fixed point! https://github.com/fixed-point-fndsa/fxp-falcon-py

chat.author: Thom Wiggers
chat.text: depending on how they phrase the FN-DSA spec they could allow or ban fixed-point implementations... (it has some implications on which public/private keys are valid)

chat.author: Bas Westerbaan
chat.text:
Thom Wiggers said:

depending on how they phrase the FN-DSA spec they could allow or ban fixed-point implementations... (it has some implications on which public/private keys are valid)

Using the safe fixed-point implementation for CAVP test vectors would be great.

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: lol.  just one more thing super quick

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: and it's a combiner :)

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: lol

chat.author: Bas Westerbaan
chat.text: Having one seed as private key format is nice, as it would prevent the state from being put in the private key

chat.author: Andrew Dolan
chat.text: I'm interested in the use case alignment of this one with the statefulness aspect of LMS.

chat.author: Thom Wiggers
chat.text: one of these options is clearly more elite than then others

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: back to "what's the use case"

chat.author: Bas Westerbaan
chat.text: If FN-DSA is fixed, you can have seeds.

chat.author: Andrew Dolan
chat.text: Constrained devices doing state management of the LMS key?

chat.author: Michael Richardson
chat.text: If you cant use seeds, then our big debate goes away... I think that the PQ/PQ situation is interesting for constrained devices that want to skip hybrid disco/quantum-safe algorithms.

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: Andrew: i think it's for constrained verifiers.

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: not constrained signers.

chat.author: Michael Richardson
chat.text:
Michael Richardson said:

If you cant use seeds, then our big debate goes away... I think that the PQ/PQ situation is interesting for constrained devices that want to skip hybrid disco/quantum-safe algorithms.

But, I'm not sure it simpler than LMS alone, which for many situations, is much faster.

chat.author: Alicja Kario
chat.text: there is no side-stepping of the state issue...

chat.author: Morgane Guerreau
chat.text: I don't know about this specific FN-DSA+LMS combination but there are use-cases for PQ/PQ

chat.author: Jan Klaußner
chat.text: I also lik the FN-DSA+LMS idea

chat.author: Tadahiko Ito
chat.text: Certificate is small...
